topic: What the highest-performing Sanad offices in Oman are doing differently from the average office right now
format: Walk through the exact day-in-the-life of a Sanad office manager handling 20+ client companies with no case tracking system — every painful step in sequence
post:
A work permit expired yesterday. You found out this morning — from the client.

Now someone else wants an update on a visa renewal you submitted three weeks ago. You open the shared Excel. Scroll. Hope someone remembered to update it. Another client calls while you're doing this. You let it ring.

This is most of the 924 licensed Sanad offices in Oman right now.

The offices pulling ahead aren't bigger or better staffed. They just stopped running everything through message threads and memory.

Every work permit, every visa, every MOL filing tracked per client company in one place. Expiry alerts go out before anyone has to chase. Each PRO officer has a clear view of their actual workload. Clients check their own request status and stop calling to ask.

Same team. 40+ client companies. No expired permits discovered by accident. No 8 AM damage control calls.

The problem isn't your people. It's what you're asking them to work with.

WhatsApp and Excel aren't a workflow. They're a system where whoever messages loudest gets attention first — and everything quiet slips through.

Try the Sanad AI Assistant free — instant answers on work permits, visas & government fees: www.thesmartpro.io/sanad/assistant
